Notice Of Motion To Disqualify Chritine Tour-Sarkissian, Paul Tour-Sarkission, Phil Foster, Abigail Morris And The Tour-Sarkissian Law Offices & Tour-Sarkissian Law Offices, Llp From Representing James Cantrell And Cantrell, Harris & Associates, Inc.
Off Calendar Aug-06-2012 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. PHIL FOSTER OF THE TOUR SARKISSIAN LAW OFFICE, TEL. no. 626-7744 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF CHRISTINA SAGONOWSKY'S Motion To Disqualify Chritine Tour-Sarkissian, Paul Tour-Sarkission, Phil Foster, Abigail Morris And The Tour-Sarkissian Law Offices & Tour-Sarkissian Law Offices, Llp From Representing James Cantrell And Cantrell, Harris & Associates, Inc is Denied. Plaintiff did not have a reasonable expectation that her former counsel would withhold information from the present client. Vapnek, Tuft, et al., Cal. Practice Guide: Prof'l Resp., 4:179. The present client was previously retained as an expert witness who testified at trial in the underlying action, which is the subject of the present litigation. The types of communication from the former case at issue (see discussion at Opposition at 9 et seq.) relate to this case and will in any event be disclosed. Styles v. Mumbert, 164 Cal.App.4th 1163, 1167 (2008) is not of assistance to Plaintiff as that (1) states the general rule, an exception to which is that obtaining here and stated in the first sentence of this ruling, and (2) in contrast to Styles, this is, in fact, a malpractice case. Compare id. at 1168. Plaintiff's request for judicial notice is unopposed and is accordingly granted. Plaintiff's supplemental request for judicial notice filed in support of the reply is improper, and is thus denied. Objections to the Morris Declaration are overruled. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge Curtis Karnow; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported.
